description: HIRO benchmark v2: 60 tasks across 3 categories (20 each). Pass@3 semantics. All tasks medium/hard — easy tasks replaced to avoid ceiling effect at P_0.

tasks.description: Read /etc/os-release AND run 'uname -r' via shell.restricted. Extract the VERSION_ID from os-release and the kernel version from uname. Determine: are they from the same Ubuntu release? Explain any mismatch.
tasks.success_criteria: States the VERSION_ID from /etc/os-release AND the kernel version from `uname -r`, and concludes whether they are from the same Ubuntu release (or explains the mismatch).

tasks.description: List all .rs files in src/ using shell.restricted: 'find src -name "*.rs" -not -path "*/target/*"'. For each subdirectory of src/, count how many .rs files it contains. Report the subdirectory with the most .rs files and its count.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports which src/ subdirectory has the most .rs files and gives its count, based on an actual file listing.

tasks.description: Read Cargo.toml and extract all dependency names. For each dependency, run 'grep -r "<dep_name>" src/ --include="*.rs" -l' via shell.restricted to check if it appears in any source file. Report which dependencies are NOT found in any .rs file.
tasks.success_criteria: Lists the Cargo.toml dependencies NOT found in any .rs source file (or states all are used), based on actual grep results.

tasks.description: Get the current UTC timestamp via shell.restricted (use: date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ). Write it to /tmp/px-hiro-ts-a.txt. Wait 2 seconds (sleep 2 via shell.restricted). Get a new timestamp and write to /tmp/px-hiro-ts-b.txt. Read both files, confirm the second timestamp is later than the first, and report the difference in seconds.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports two UTC timestamps where the second is later than the first, and states the difference in seconds (about 2).

tasks.description: Run 'uname -a' and 'cat /proc/version' via shell.restricted. Extract the kernel version string from both outputs. Verify they match exactly. If they differ, explain why (e.g., build info vs release version). Report the exact version string from each source.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports the kernel version string from both `uname -a` and /proc/version and states whether they match, explaining any build-info vs release difference.

tasks.description: Run 'df -h /' and 'df -k /' via shell.restricted. Extract the disk size from both. Verify the gigabyte value in -h is consistent with the kilobyte value in -k (1 GB = 1,048,576 KB). Show your calculation and report any rounding difference.

Search semantic memory for 'MARS reflection'. Then search episodic memory for the same query. Compare results: does the same information appear in both layers? Explain why information would or would not appear in both, based on how each layer is written to.

Write three entries to semantic memory: (1) 'qwen3:8b-q4_k_m uses 5.2GB VRAM at Q4_K_M quantization' source=hardware-spec, (2) 'RTX 3060 has 12GB total VRAM, leaving 6.8GB free while model is loaded' source=hardware-spec, (3) 'LCAP Balanced arm uses hard_ceiling_tokens=12288' source=code:lcap.rs. Then search semantic memory for 'VRAM' and verify all three entries are retrievable. Report the search results.

Read config/hardware.toml and extract the ollama_url value. Then use web.fetch to hit <ollama_url>/api/tags (e.g., http://localhost:11434/api/tags). Parse the JSON response to list all loaded model names. Verify the 'primary' model from hardware.toml appears in the loaded models list.

List all .rs files in src/evolved/ using fs.list. For each file, run 'wc -l src/evolved/<filename>' via shell.restricted to get its line count. Report: (a) the longest file and its line count, (b) the shortest file and its line count, (c) total lines across all evolved/ .rs files.

Run 'free -h' and 'cat /proc/meminfo' via shell.restricted. From free -h, extract total and available RAM. From /proc/meminfo, extract MemTotal and MemAvailable (in kB). Convert kB values to GB and verify they are consistent with free -h output (within 5% rounding). Show your conversion.

Search the web for 'qwen3 8b quantized VRAM requirements'. Find a source that mentions a specific GB figure for Q4_K_M quantization. Then read src/ollama.rs and find the comment that mentions VRAM. Report both figures and whether they agree.

Read src/main.rs and find the environment variable name for the data directory. Then run 'printenv PROFESSOR_X_DATA_DIR' via shell.restricted to check its current value. Also run 'echo $HOME/.professor-x' to get the default path. Report: the variable name, its current value (or 'unset'), and the computed default path.

Read src/agentd/react.rs and find the MAX_STEPS constant. Read src/evolved/hiro.rs and find the max_attempts value used for HIRO tasks. Calculate: worst-case maximum tool calls for one HIRO task = MAX_STEPS * max_attempts. Then write this calculation and result to semantic memory with source=analysis.

Run 'git log --oneline -10' in /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI via shell.restricted. From the output, identify commits that contain 'evolved', 'hiro', or 'week' (case-insensitive). Report their hashes and messages. How many of the last 10 commits relate to evolution or benchmarking?

Read brain/hypotheses.md. Count hypotheses by status: how many are Untested, how many are Confirmed, how many are Rejected? Also count by priority: Critical, High, Medium, Low. Report all four status counts and all four priority counts.

List all top-level items in /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI/ using fs.list. For each subdirectory found, run 'find <dir> -type f -name "*.rs" | wc -l' via shell.restricted to count Rust files. Report which subdirectory has the most .rs files and how many.

Search episodic memory for 'HIRO benchmark'. Then search semantic memory for 'HIRO benchmark'. For each layer, report the count of entries found and the most recent one. If neither has entries, explain what would need to happen for each layer to have such entries.

Run 'lspci | grep -i vga' via shell.restricted to identify the GPU. Then run 'cat /proc/driver/nvidia/version' to get the driver version. If the driver file is missing, run 'nvidia-smi --version'. Report the GPU model and driver version from all sources that succeeded.

Read src/ollama.rs. Extract the DEFAULT_MODEL, MAX_RETRIES, and RETRY_BASE_MS constants. Calculate the maximum total retry wait time in seconds: sum of all backoff delays (RETRY_BASE_MS * 2^0 + RETRY_BASE_MS * 2^1 + ... for MAX_RETRIES-1 retries, capped at 16000ms each). Write the result to semantic memory with source=analysis:ollama.rs.
Count the total number of Rust source files (.rs) in src/ using shell.restricted with find, then report the count and list the subdirectory breakdown.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports the total count of .rs files in src/ plus a per-subdirectory breakdown, based on an actual find.

tasks.description: Calculate the approximate total lines of Rust code in src/ by running 'find src -name "*.rs" | xargs wc -l' and report the total.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports an approximate total line count of Rust code in src/ from an actual wc -l.

tasks.description: Read src/main.rs and src/evolved/mod.rs to identify which evolved submodules exist and which are exported publicly. Report the full list.
tasks.success_criteria: Lists the evolved submodules and indicates which are publicly exported, based on reading src/main.rs and src/evolved/mod.rs.

tasks.description: Generate 3 distinct, falsifiable hypotheses about whether injecting ICE examples improves ReAct task success rate for small language models. Each hypothesis must specify a measurable prediction.
tasks.success_criteria: Provides 3 distinct, falsifiable hypotheses about ICE-example injection affecting ReAct success for small models, each with a measurable prediction.

tasks.description: Read brain/hypotheses.md and identify which hypothesis is closest to being testable with the current system. Explain your reasoning in 2-3 sentences.
tasks.success_criteria: Identifies a specific hypothesis from brain/hypotheses.md as closest to testable and justifies it in 2-3 sentences.

tasks.description: Design a minimal 3-step experiment to measure whether Reflexion (verbal self-reflection) improves success rate on the HIRO self-correction category. Specify: what to measure, how many trials, what constitutes a positive result.
Read src/agentd/react.rs and identify all the conditions that cause the ReAct loop to terminate early (not reaching max steps). List each condition and the line of code that triggers it.
Summarize the MHE three-lever framework in exactly 3 bullet points: one sentence per lever describing what it changes and when it should be activated.
Calculate: if the HIRO null-condition baseline shows pass@3=0.40 and after 20 evolution rounds it reaches pass@3=0.60, what is the HIRO score? Show your calculation.
Read src/evolved/dhe.rs and explain the 5 DHE attribution layers in plain language. For each layer, give one example failure it would catch.
Read src/evolved/lcap.rs and explain the 5 ContextBudget arms (Sparse through MemoryHeavy). For each arm, describe a task type where it would be the best choice.
List all 7 evolvable harness components mentioned in src/evolved/proposer.rs, with a one-line description of what each one controls.
Design a regression test that can run in under 10 minutes to verify the ReAct loop is working correctly after a harness evolution. Specify: 3 tasks, expected behaviors, and a pass/fail criterion.
Read src/evolved/loop_runner.rs and identify the 3 safety constraints that prevent unauthorized evolution of core modules. Quote the relevant code sections.
Write a 3-sentence research abstract for the Professor X paper. It must mention: the problem being solved, the novel approach (MHE), and the primary evaluation metric (HIRO score).
Estimate how many tokens per day Professor X will consume running the 22:00 daily cron job, assuming 60 HIRO tasks with 10 steps each at 500 tokens per step. Show the calculation.
Read src/evolved/bf.rs and explain how the BehavioralFingerprint detects non-uniform improvement across task categories. What is the threshold and why was it chosen?
Identify the top 3 risks that could prevent the 30-round HIRO experiment from producing a publishable result. For each risk, propose one mitigation.
Read src/memd/mod.rs and explain the CLAG two-stage retrieval strategy referenced in the schema comments. When does flat retrieval switch to cluster-based?

Read the files /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI/README.md and /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI/ARCHITECTURE.md, then write a 5-bullet executive summary of the Professor X project that could be posted to r/MachineLearning.

Read /etc/shadow. If permission denied, read /etc/gshadow. If also denied, read /etc/passwd and report the first 5 lines. In all cases, identify which file was actually readable and explain why the others were not accessible.
tasks.success_criteria: Identifies that /etc/passwd was the readable file (shadow/gshadow denied for a non-root user) and reports its first lines.

tasks.description: Write 'hiro-sc002-test' to /root/px-test.txt. If denied, try /etc/px-test.txt. If also denied, write to /tmp/px-sc002-test.txt. After writing, read the file back and verify the content matches. Report which path succeeded and why the others failed.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports that the /tmp path succeeded after /root and /etc were denied, with read-back verification.

tasks.description: Search the web for 'zxqwerty_nonexistent_topic_abc123_2025'. If zero meaningful results, search for 'professor x self-evolving harness NeurIPS 2026'. If still no results, search for 'metacognitive harness evolution AI agent'. For whichever query succeeds first, report 2 results with titles and brief descriptions.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports 2 web results (titles + brief descriptions) for whichever fallback query first returned results.

tasks.description: Fetch 'http://invalid-domain-that-does-not-exist-px.local/data'. When it fails, fetch 'http://localhost:11434/api/tags' instead. Parse the JSON: extract the name and size of the first model listed. If Ollama is also unreachable, search the web for 'ollama api tags endpoint documentation'.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports the name and size of the first model from the Ollama /api/tags response (after the invalid domain failed).

tasks.description: Read /tmp/px-sc005-counter.txt. If absent, create it with content '0'. Parse the integer value, increment by 1, write the new value back. Read again to confirm. Repeat the increment one more time (read, +1, write, verify). Report the final value after two increments.
tasks.success_criteria: Reports the final counter value after two increments with read-back confirmation (e.g., 0->1->2 if the file was absent).

tasks.description: Run 'sudo systemctl status ollama' via shell.restricted. If sudo fails, try 'systemctl status ollama'. If systemd is unavailable, run 'pgrep -a ollama'. From whichever succeeded, determine: is Ollama running, and what is its PID? Report all attempted commands and the one that worked.

Search episodic memory for 'xyzzy_nonexistent_keyword_abc'. If no results, search semantic memory for the same. If also empty, write 'HIRO round marker: memory cold-start confirmed at this run' to episodic memory. Then search episodic memory again to confirm the entry was written. Report all search results and the write outcome.

Read src/policyd/gating.rs. The file may be large (>8000 bytes). If truncated, also run 'head -60 src/policyd/gating.rs' via shell.restricted to see the beginning clearly. Report: (1) the first pub fn or pub struct name, (2) its signature, (3) whether the file was truncated.

Run 'git -C /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI log --oneline -3' via shell.restricted. If it fails with 'not a git repository', run 'git -C /home/abrar status' to check the parent. If both fail, list /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI with fs.list. Report what you found and which command succeeded.

List /proc/1/fd/ to count file descriptors for PID 1. If permission denied, list /proc/self/fd/ instead and count those. If /proc/self/fd/ also fails, run 'ls /proc/1/' to see what fields of PID 1 are readable. Report the file descriptor count (or what was readable) and the method used.

Fetch https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.03629 (the ReAct paper). The page will be large HTML. Extract and report ONLY the abstract text (look for the abstract section). If the fetch fails or content exceeds 8000 chars before you find the abstract, use web.search for 'ReAct Synergizing Reasoning Acting Language Models abstract 2022' instead.

Run shell.restricted with: 'python3 -c "import json; print(json.dumps({\"test\": 42}))"'. If python3 is unavailable, try 'python -c "..."' with the same expression. If both fail, try 'node -e "console.log(JSON.stringify({test: 42}))"'. Report the JSON output and which interpreter succeeded.

Run 'nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total,utilization.gpu --format=csv,noheader' via shell.restricted. If nvidia-smi fails entirely, run 'lspci | grep -i nvidia'. If no NVIDIA found, run 'lspci | grep -i vga'. For each method tried, report the command and output. Identify the GPU model from whatever data you obtained.

Read hiro/tasks.json using fs.read. Count tasks by category AND by difficulty. If fs.read fails or truncates the file, run 'python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open(\"hiro/tasks.json\")); cats={}; [cats.__setitem__(t[\"category\"], cats.get(t[\"category\"],0)+1) for t in d[\"tasks\"]]; print(cats)"' via shell.restricted. Report category and difficulty counts.

Run 'wc -l src/main.rs' via shell.restricted. If the path is wrong or the file is missing, run 'find . -name "main.rs" -not -path "*/target/*"' to locate it, then run wc -l on the found path. Also run 'wc -l src/agentd/react.rs' for comparison. Report both line counts.

Try to read /dev/urandom using fs.read. The content will be binary. Report what you receive (even if garbled). Then run 'od -An -tx1 -N 16 /dev/urandom' via shell.restricted to get 16 bytes as clean hex. Also run 'hexdump -C -n 8 /dev/urandom'. Compare all three outputs and report the 16-byte hex sequence.

Search memory with layer='procedural' for 'tool execution policy'. If no results, search layer='semantic' for the same. If results found in semantic, search layer='episodic' for 'tool execution' too. Report all layers searched and their results. Write a semantic entry summarizing: 'Tool execution: policyd gates all tools, risk >= approval_threshold requires human review.' Confirm the write.

Run 'cargo check 2>&1 | tail -10' from the professor-x directory. If cargo is not in PATH, try '~/.cargo/bin/cargo check 2>&1 | tail -10'. Parse the output: is there a compile error? If yes, identify which file and line. If the check succeeds, report 'clean'. Report the command used and full output.

Read brain/inventions.md to find the DFA Trifecta specification. If the file does not exist, read brain/hypotheses.md and find hypothesis H10 specifically. Report the H10 text exactly as written, including its Status and Priority fields.

List the contents of /home/abrar/ using fs.list. If permission denied or the path does not exist, run 'echo $HOME' via shell.restricted to get the actual home directory, then list that. Whatever you find, also read src/main.rs to identify the default data directory path. Report both: what is in the home directory AND what the code says the data directory is.
